generated.title: GeneratedMagneticSusceptability
generated.description: This is an autogenerated partial class definition of 'MagneticSusceptability'

description*.en: "Magnetic Susceptability" is a scalar or tensor quantity the product of which by the magnetic constant $\mu_0$ and by the magnetic field strength $H$ is equal to the magnetic polarization $J$. The definition given applies to an isotropic medium. For an anisotropic medium permeability is a second order tensor.

title: MagneticSusceptability
description: "Magnetic Susceptability" is a scalar or tensor quantity the product of which by the magnetic constant $\mu_0$ and by the magnetic field strength $H$ is equal to the magnetic polarization $J$. The definition given applies to an isotropic medium. For an anisotropic medium permeability is a second order tensor.
